entities.name: NEW TRAITS
entities.description: Desired characteristics introduced into plants via genetic modification

entities.name: TRADITIONAL BREEDING SYSTEMS
entities.description: Conventional methods of plant breeding used before genetic engineering

entities.name: GENETIC ENGINEERING
entities.description: The process of manipulating an organism's genes using biotechnology

entities.name: GENETIC ENHANCEMENT
entities.description: The improvement of a crop's genetic makeup to enhance productivity or resilience

entities.name: FOOD CROPS
entities.description: Crops grown specifically for human consumption

entities.name: TRANSFORMATION METHODS
entities.description: Techniques used to introduce foreign DNA into a host cell

entities.name: FOREIGN DNA
entities.description: Genetic material derived from a species other than the target organism

entities.name: RECOMBINANT DNA METHODS
entities.description: Techniques for combining DNA from two different sources

entities.name: TRANSGENIC PLANTS
entities.description: Plants that have been genetically modified to contain a gene from another species

entities.name: TISSUE CULTURE REGENERATION SYSTEMS
entities.description: Methods for regenerating whole plants from isolated tissues or cells

entities.name: GENE CONSTRUCTS
entities.description: Artificially assembled DNA sequences designed for expression in a target organism

entities.name: TRANSFORMATION TECHNIQUES
entities.description: Specific procedures for delivering genetic material into plant cells

entities.name: MOLECULAR AND GENETIC CHARACTERIZATION
entities.description: The process of analyzing transgenic plants to ensure stable and efficient gene expression

entities.name: CONVENTIONAL BREEDING METHODS
entities.description: Standard agricultural breeding practices used to transfer traits to elite cultivars

entities.name: BIOTIC AND ABIOTIC STRESSES
entities.description: Biological and environmental pressures that negatively impact plant growth

entities.name: ENVIRONMENTAL BIOHAZARD
entities.description: Potential biological risks posed by transgenic organisms to the environment

entities.name: GENETIC TRANSFORMATION TECHNOLOGY
entities.description: The overall technological framework for modifying plant genetics

entities.name: HETEROLOGOUS SPECIES
entities.description: Species that are different from the target crop species
